name: Archdiocese of Birmingham
coverage_note: The Archdiocese of Birmingham is divided into the three Pastoral Areas of Birmingham, Kidderminster and Worcester.

sources.title: Birmingham profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Archdiocese of Liverpool
coverage_note: The Archdiocese of Liverpool covers the city itself and the surrounding areas including Wigan, St Helens, Southport and the Isle of Man.

sources.title: Liverpool profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Archdiocese of Southwark
coverage_note: The Archdiocese of Southwark covers the London boroughs south of the Thames, the County of Kent and the Medway Unitary Authority.

sources.title: Southwark profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Archdiocese of Westminster
coverage_note: Home to Westminster Cathedral, the mother church for Catholics in England and Wales, the Diocese of Westminster has 214 parishes.

sources.title: Westminster profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Arundel and Brighton
coverage_note: The Diocese of Arundel and Brighton serves the counties of Sussex and those parts of Surrey outside the Greater London Boroughs.

sources.title: Arundel and Brighton profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Brentwood
coverage_note: Official diocesan route for England.

sources.title: Brentwood profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Clifton
coverage_note: The Diocese of Clifton covers the West of England and includes the City and County of Bristol, the counties of Gloucestershire, Somerset, Wiltshire, North Somerset, South Gloucestershire and Bath and North East Somerset.

sources.title: Clifton profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of East Anglia
coverage_note: The Diocese of East Anglia spans the Counties of Norfolk, Suffolk, Cambridgeshire and the Unitary Authority of Peterborough.

sources.title: East Anglia profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Hallam
coverage_note: The Diocese of Hallam was formed in May 1980 after the division of the Dioceses of Leeds and Nottingham.

sources.title: Hallam profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Hexham and Newcastle
coverage_note: The Diocese of Hexham and Newcastle covers a large part of the North East including the counties of Northumberland, Tyne and Wear and Durham.

sources.title: Hexham and Newcastle profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Lancaster
coverage_note: The Diocese of Lancaster extends along the west of England from the Ribble River in the south of Preston to the Scottish border, comprising the counties of Cumbria and much of Lancashire.

sources.title: Lancaster profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Leeds
coverage_note: The Diocese of Leeds comprises the whole of West Yorkshire together with parishes in the East Riding, North Yorkshire, Greater Manchester and Lancashire.

sources.title: Leeds profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Middlesbrough
coverage_note: The Diocese of Middlesbrough consists of the boroughs of Middlesbrough, Redcar and Cleveland, Stockton on Tees (south of the river), the cities of Kingston upon Hull and York, East Yorkshire and most of North Yorkshire.

sources.title: Middlesbrough profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Northampton
coverage_note: The Diocese of Northampton consists of the counties of Bedford, Buckingham and Northampton. The town of Slough also falls within the diocesan boundary.

sources.title: Northampton profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Nottingham
coverage_note: The Diocese of Nottingham was founded in 1850 and covers the counties of Nottinghamshire, Derbyshire, Leicestershire and Rutland, and Lincolnshire.

sources.title: Nottingham profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Plymouth
coverage_note: The Diocese of Plymouth covers almost the whole of south-west England, serving Catholics in Devon, Dorset and Cornwall - including the Isles of Scilly.

sources.title: Plymouth profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Portsmouth
coverage_note: Official diocesan route for England.

sources.title: Portsmouth profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Salford
coverage_note: The diocese sits in the north-west of England and centres on the city of Salford, a large industrial centre that neighbours Manchester.

sources.title: Salford profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

name: Diocese of Shrewsbury
coverage_note: The Diocese of Shrewsbury encompasses parts of the North West of England and parts of the West Midlands. The bishop is the Right Reverend Mark Davies.

sources.title: Shrewsbury profile | Catholic Bishops' Conference of England and Wales

coverage_note: The Archdiocese of Cardiff-Menevia has several deaneries covering Cardiff, the Welsh valleys, Bridgend, Hereford, Newport, North Gwent, Pontypridd, Swansea, Carmarthen, Llandrindod wells, Haverfordwest and Port Talbot.

coverage_note: The Diocese of Wrexham covers North Wales - the areas surrounding Caernarfon, Colwyn Bay, Dolgellau, Flint, Rhyl and of course, Wrexham.
